skill.name: pptx
skill.description: Create, inspect, edit, validate, and thumbnail PowerPoint presentations using HTML-to-PPTX and Office Open XML workflows.
skill.summary: Guides AI agents through PowerPoint creation, editing, validation, and visual review workflows.
skill.author: ComposioHQ

security_audit.summary: AI review confirmed a real unsafe archive extraction risk in helper scripts that process user-supplied Office files. Most static hits for weak cryptography, C2 keywords, hardcoded URLs, and JavaScript backticks are false positives from OOXML schemas, examples, and template strings. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious intent was found, but publication should wait for zip-slip hardening.

high_findings.title: Unsafe Archive Extraction of Office Files
high_findings.description: Multiple helpers call extractall on PPTX, DOCX, or original Office archives without validating member paths. A crafted ZIP-based Office file can write outside the intended temporary or output directory through zip-slip paths.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly extracts user-controlled Office archives with zipfile.extractall and no path validation. The file format is a ZIP container, so zip-slip payloads are a realistic threat.

medium_findings.title: External Tool Execution on User-Supplied Documents
medium_findings.description: The scripts invoke soffice, pdftoppm, and git through subprocess.run. Arguments are passed as lists, which reduces shell injection risk, but the tools still parse user-provided presentation files and can expand the runtime attack surface.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess calls are real and operate on user-influenced file paths, but they do not use shell=True or concatenate shell commands. This is an elevated processing risk rather than clear command injection.

medium_findings.title: Privileged Dependency Installation Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill documentation recommends sudo apt-get commands for LibreOffice and Poppler. This does not execute automatically, but marketplace users could be guided toward privileged package installation during skill setup.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sudo commands are explicit in the dependency section. They are setup guidance, not hidden execution, so the concern is user safety and review friction.

low_findings.title: Benign Environment and Local Filesystem Access
low_findings.description: The static scanner flagged TMPDIR, font directories, and output writes. Reviewed usage is consistent with temporary rendering, font discovery, and writing generated inventory files for presentation workflows.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The accesses are real but have a clear local processing purpose. I did not find evidence that environment values or local files are exfiltrated.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Schemas and Template Strings
low_findings.description: Many weak-crypto, C2, path traversal, network, and JavaScript backtick detections are schema terms, XML namespace URLs, relative schema imports, color names, or template literals rather than malicious behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed examples show normal OOXML schema imports, schema element names, JavaScript template strings, and color palette text. These do not establish command-and-control, cryptographic misuse, or obfuscation.

dangerous_patterns.title: zipfile.extractall Without Path Validation
dangerous_patterns.description: Archive extraction is performed directly on Office ZIP containers. Replace this with safe member validation that rejects absolute paths and entries that resolve outside the target directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact dangerous API appears at each listed location, and no surrounding path validation was observed in the reviewed code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Privileged Install Commands in User-Facing Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The dependency list includes sudo apt-get examples. Marketplace packaging should avoid privileged commands or clearly move them to administrator-reviewed installation documentation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command text is present and user-facing. It is not malicious by itself, but it is a dangerous pattern for an AI skill marketplace.

content.value_statement: PowerPoint work often needs precise layout control and safe OOXML edits. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through creation, editing, validation, and review workflows.
actual_capabilities: Create new PowerPoint decks from HTML slide layouts using the included html2pptx workflow.
Inspect presentation text, slide XML, comments, speaker notes, layouts, masters, themes, and media files.
Edit existing PPTX files by unpacking Office Open XML, changing slide XML, validating, and repacking.
Generate thumbnail grids for visual review of slide layout, text clipping, contrast, and positioning.
Use existing template decks by inventorying slides, selecting layouts, and replacing placeholder content.
Convert supporting PDF pages to images for presentation assets when required dependencies are available.
limitations: The helper scripts process local files and require Python, Node.js, LibreOffice, Poppler, and related packages.
The current archive extraction helpers need zip-slip hardening before broad marketplace publication.
Complex animations, embedded media behavior, and proprietary PowerPoint features may need manual review.
Visual fidelity depends on installed fonts, rendering tools, and careful thumbnail inspection.

use_cases.title: Build a New Client Deck
use_cases.description: Create a structured PPTX from provided content, choose a visual direction, and validate slide thumbnails before delivery.

use_cases.title: Update an Existing Presentation
use_cases.description: Modify text, notes, comments, and slide XML while preserving layouts, themes, and template structure.

use_cases.title: Audit Template Assets
use_cases.description: Inspect a template deck, inventory slide layouts, identify reusable patterns, and prepare replacement guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Deck
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a five-slide PowerPoint about [topic]. Use a clear structure, professional visuals, and generate thumbnails for review.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a Presentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this PPTX file. Summarize the slide content, speaker notes, theme colors, fonts, and any visible layout issues.

prompt_templates.title: Edit a Template Deck
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this template PPTX to create a new deck for [audience]. Inventory the template, select suitable layouts, and replace placeholder content.

prompt_templates.title: Repair and Validate OOXML
prompt_templates.prompt: Unpack this PPTX, edit the requested slide XML, validate after each change, repack the file, and create thumbnails to confirm the result.

output_examples.input: Create a quarterly business review deck from these notes.
output: A finished PPTX with title, agenda, performance, risks, and next-step slides.
A thumbnail grid used to check layout, contrast, and text fit.
A short summary of design choices and any remaining manual review items.

output_examples.input: Update the existing sales deck with new pricing and speaker notes.
output: An edited presentation that keeps the original theme and slide structure.
A validation summary for the modified Office XML files.
A note listing changed slides and any content that needs stakeholder approval.

output_examples.input: Analyze this template and tell me which slides to reuse.
output: A slide inventory grouped by purpose, such as title, section divider, data, and closing slides.
Recommendations for matching each content section to a template layout.

best_practices: Inspect the source deck or template before editing so layouts, colors, and fonts are preserved.
Validate and thumbnail the deck after each meaningful change to catch broken XML and visual regressions.
Use local copies of trusted files and review generated slides before sharing them externally.
anti_patterns: Do not process untrusted Office files with the current extraction helpers until path validation is added.
Do not skip thumbnail review when slides contain charts, dense text, or precise positioning.
Do not run privileged dependency installation commands without administrator review and approval.

faq.question: Can this skill create new PPTX files?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses an HTML-to-PPTX workflow and PptxGenJS to create slides with controlled layout and styling.

faq.question: Can it edit an existing presentation?
faq.answer: Yes. It can unpack a PPTX, edit Office Open XML files, validate changes, and repack the presentation.

faq.question: Does it support speaker notes and comments?
faq.answer: Yes. The instructions cover raw XML access for speaker notes, comments, slide layouts, masters, themes, and media.

faq.question: What tools does it require?
faq.answer: It expects Python, Node.js packages, LibreOffice, Poppler, markitdown, playwright, sharp, and defusedxml for full workflows.

faq.question: Is it safe for untrusted PPTX files?
faq.answer: Not yet. The audit found unsafe archive extraction that should be fixed before processing untrusted Office files.

faq.question: Can it guarantee PowerPoint visual fidelity?
faq.answer: No. It improves fidelity with validation and thumbnails, but final results still depend on fonts, rendering tools, and manual review.
